snippet: The 2026 Java stack just added a whole new set of layers. View in browser Денис, For today's guest post, we brought back Javin Paul — Java developer, technical educator, and author of a newsletter
subject: If you're a Java dev, you're gonna want to read this
body:
The 2026 Java stack just added a whole new set of layers.

View in browser (https://learn.educative.io/e3t/Ctc/RG+113/cz5H404/VVFMRJ2wznFgW4h-lf27dQTk9W8GDvjX5KN9ywN9k7Mbb5m_5PW7Y9pgv6lZ3l4W2N5L_b47HFdnW2W3Z0P1QqGJQV7HV-r6zdVlBW6J9y9_4MJJr0VGYkhf1NHw9hW4QCvzH8k-jf9W2wRcl08HsxlTW8d_MQX2wNNvPMNn3nZZ9m3rW5p2tls293PNwW5TNSB86fM2_SW6Xtf823bg9HfW5612JF78sqVTW276cV07wQD93W3tc94B4nq8-5W3m3JCC6tVMLtVvmXv61SPK39N7Y-qPWDk0JwW488w5d49QpVGW8T9-H71cNTvpW8jFQY978_97yW5P0ty49fFXR9N33Hddr9hszZN36N63KCk5JjW91rcht43frwCW2zQjQJ8K9PcGW6r9PDc1qY-L5W1Y8z387Kjm0nW2VYqr51BPVt6W3MQtGD1YsyJKW89RVwX4rvcRRN2YFfW2KZDvZN86rxV1sHk2QW9fZq5F4VxCwnW3L5z0N4_02HxVyL3YX5jZVsrW4p0CCB2qTkTVW4d56vP5HS-Z4W4qyGbL3_RNgpW5WQzsb7z4nxHW8b3ZGq77Y_BZW6Zc4xQ1zjSJDf4Nt2fl04 )

Денис,

For today's guest post, we brought back Javin Paul — Java developer, technical educator, and author of a newsletter read by 44,000+ engineers at Google, Amazon, Meta, and more. He's been writing about Java and developer careers since 2010, and today he's sharing the 8 skills he thinks every Java dev needs to stay ahead in 2026.

Whether you're brushing up or leveling up, Educative's hands-on courses can help you practice each one: no setup, no videos, just real coding in your browser. (And as fate would have it, our flash sale was just extended (https://learn.educative.io/e3t/Ctc/RG+113/cz5H404/VVFMRJ2wznFgW4h-lf27dQTk9W8GDvjX5KN9ywN9k7Mbv3prCCW8wLKSR6lZ3nHW5tnxn880sV2RW7Wrp1_48MJpwW4YKwQ711-jZBW605-Xx6xjnGKN4F49J7gVbtJN9fH22CCZGJzN6wG71j-Rn_5Vvg3kj57jP4_W5CXMGV3VjV-3W2Pyfrs68S4TjW7GyGK76dfzR-W1_cSGq2SSJDjW3LrfGT1LNRNWW5y_-gk3Kbk88W7JK30F61ZQtxW3t8yzc8nSq8lV8_pnw4q_2r7W6HGYKg6gGVH3V6KY5C27CsvSW8pSZDf5p4VkVW7w0VvJ21L3ZTW8bG88B6CchYlW8JFzkk1Tkf6lW4-Hzhk34dP3HVHFTtr91jsR4W34rfs64jTlwXW8xdBD496dld7W7MMlfJ7mYfKyf4zvZQY04 ) for a few more hours 👀)

Over to you, Javin!

Hello guys, Javin here. If you don’t know me, I am a Java developer and have been coding for the last 21 years. I have also been writing since 2010 and have written multiple articles on Java, Programming and Coding interviews.

I’m also the creator of the Javarevisited newsletter (https://learn.educative.io/e3t/Ctc/RG+113/cz5H404/VVFMRJ2wznFgW4h-lf27dQTk9W8GDvjX5KN9ywN9k7Mbb3prCCW7Y8-PT6lZ3mtW80RtNJ4rQCwSW4QbbWg8vRzvkW7D65181VhjCpW4R9zhH12PBpRVpLTq34Xt07DW3M8Qpy1mQfFrW31CNnV97cS2kW1Qw44G1pM0GtW22fG6C1htPdNW8nkhvB99W2FHW4XlCdW5TmGtnW7yLQfV7gWmfFVlkGFF2L3jLGN1c_swbWRX1DW7Nvyhp2bjkGGW4nV6085Cbp1kVmT5sK7htb1PVfY0JT6Qw9xbN8pHC5z1TYmzMSX5-HMHfxSW3hN4dC29G2jnW2W5SLJ67Z_DPW5hn6Kt2YCj4RVHWXZ57pV4QjW6N19bd3f4QzpW1vCRL-16dbhsf6mytxR04 ) , where I share interview tips, questions, in-depth articles and resources to 52,001+ engineers from Infosys, IGoogle, Meta, Amazon, Investment banks, and startups. If you are preparing for Java developer interviews, you can also check out my books on Java interviews on Gumroad (https://learn.educative.io/e3t/Ctc/RG+113/cz5H404/VVFMRJ2wznFgW4h-lf27dQTk9W8GDvjX5KN9ywN9k7Mbv3prCCW8wLKSR6lZ3nLN74_Mjc9q8c8W5nmkQD2-YT_JW11Z-cq2JwbdDW5Nn2gY33wtb4W1YKD352nc4pQW2QfmjB5dGTfrW2_frxb7DlhS3W1zY4SD1Hsp_xW58QfZM2rklj4W21v1L-592zHXW74Gs5G5tQ_g4W10VwjS94b0f5W4Gxwcx6kPJqlW1l0-C08YYtF-W45n1XZ5tLvrVW24YJrJ6bYq2cVcStD86RWjrBW5P7GHh8ZqktjTbBFl5ncC7HW6GpJJL8JB96HW4_-8s44nxdjgN8PQQh9zhwGlW48rq3Y3z0GJbVK_0NT3YXdpTW6bRX9f8msv3JW88g1G05G47mMW2zbmcX5dDYr2W8Bb1DD1qmL4_f8g2gM804 ) .

I'm thrilled to partner with Educative this week to share the What Every Java Developer Should Learn in 2026 to Stay Relevant.

Java continues to be one of the most widely used languages in enterprise systems, cloud platforms, and large-scale applications. But being a successful Java developer in 2026 requires much more than knowing the language and its standard libraries.

Companies now expect engineers to understand architecture, scalability, cloud infrastructure, AI assisted development, and modern delivery practices.

As a Java developer, the biggest challenge you face is keeping your skills up-to-date. If you’re not actively learning, you’re falling behind.

Over the past couple of years, we’ve seen major releases like Java 25, Spring Boot 4, Spring Framework 7, Spring Security 7, and, more recently, exciting additions like Spring AI and LangChain4j, which bring AI capabilities directly into Java applications.

If you’re wondering what to focus on in 2026, here’s a curated list of essential topics every modern Java developer should explore.

8 Things Java Developers Should Learn in 2026

Without further ado, here are the 16 things Java programmers can learn in 2026 to level up their tech skills and boost their careers. The list includes tools, frameworks, libraries, as well as skills a Java developer can learn to stay relevant in 2026 and the future.

1. System Design and Architecture (Top Priority)

System design is no longer just for senior engineers. Even mid-level roles now expect candidates to design scalable, reliable systems and explain tradeoffs clearly.

Java developers should learn how to design APIs, databases, caching strategies, messaging systems, and fault-tolerant architectures.

Understanding scalability, availability, consistency, and performance is essential for both interviews and real production work.

And, if you need resources, I highly recommend joining Grokking the Modern System Design Interview (https://learn.educative.io/e3t/Ctc/RG+113/cz5H404/VVFMRJ2wznFgW4h-lf27dQTk9W8GDvjX5KN9ywN9k7M9C5m_5PW6N1X8z6lZ3m_N2HntdtQQKX5W8B7G0_85zGzMW5_kjvr932LkmW5_q_X899PQn4W4LQ4y23pY9JmW4PLnRb5FPmx5N5_2w5lxcv7jW2m03jP75P1dCW504h657hRwZzW6ZRmc08kz97YW1_R9Kt23KP7rVFLfcz8xR0rbW97z7Ds2t9hgPW6Fv7gP7R4svfW46q6_w2PRptQW2H0SLn3xBWH5N6TvG_2Nj5ZkW6S5SVB7_BvpJM8jvlkXVQ8TW6L2fJg5m25nDW45k2dK4qQTKJW55tdn63t62xcW7yS4QW8QcjGHW818GCf6mpf70W1b1DD58H6SfWW1CCdj_2Lf2HsW7993M466F3NzW5LYjyM4Qp9KHW2J-7V820J8WvN5bw9Lq1Qv8VW3_q07_5N1sFwW2XqfJK9d_QfJW91Kns43Bq2LZW2s_-FB48kGbBW70B7TZ2kZ5LjW6Qp-y_2PX9NBW1btlH26dlRJBW7wD5sC2YVmfwbCpl04 ) , one of the best resources not just to prepare for a System design interview but also to learn System Design and Software Architecture in depth.

2. Java 25 (LTS)

Java 25 is the current Long-Term Support (LTS) release, and it’s time to adopt it. It brings pattern matching, record patterns, virtual threads (Project Loom), structured concurrency, and many other enhancements.

If you’re still on Java 8 or 11, or 17this is your cue to catch up. Java 21 is feature-rich and production-ready.

And, if you want to learn Java in depth and need resources, I recommend going through Java (Core & Advanced):  The All-in-One Guide to Java Programming (https://learn.educative.io/e3t/Ctc/RG+113/cz5H404/VVFMRJ2wznFgW4h-lf27dQTk9W8GDvjX5KN9ywN9k7M905m_5PW5BWr2F6lZ3pGW5vcwLJ8MFblLVWgMRy7R2Yx6W8TJXDf5V8lqhW9dl0Jk6rWq2kW3L60vq5_W69MW5Bc-ps5VckcpN2w0kJfmp_JhW7LXPSy6Jl5zbW4vP8bn5n24-XW3xyw7Y44661sW2tl0Sp6q7PvXW8T2dxJ8J8P6zW4fzlxS2FwGBSW6m61Zk8sf8csW8F2r_t8Sx_qDW3MVjPP1K_5JWW54wWg03pSwf6VQbNql17v1WfW47b5sh5LXtbBN42WST1MBv_VW5QGfPj5vKM4kW5t9YKz6Z8n1CW6n6RkF2SGN-8W26ynZb3W9YBsVDQ5Nx6dkh5KW62c3ZZ5KmMtDW6bSglg6rdTxBW5MGy3m6Q7GZWW3L4YMg7SDpNHW8nQF0Z2KZBf_W8rmhb_5d69vsW5Gj9Qb75kFfrW1rqHvM9gD7pQVGwZr_2f25jWf8-rLRn04 ) . This isn’t a course, it’s advanced consulting disguised as education.

3. Master Spring Boot 4 and Spring Framework 7

Spring Framework 7 and Spring Boot 4, both generally available as of late 2026, introduce significant enhancements focused on modernization, performance, and developer experience.

Key new features include built-in API versioning, enhanced GraalVM native image support, and a Java 17+ baseline

If you need a course to learn Java and Spring together, I recommend going through this Spring / Spring Boot: The Complete Guide to Spring 6 and Spring Boot 3 (https://learn.educative.io/e3t/Ctc/RG+113/cz5H404/VVFMRJ2wznFgW4h-lf27dQTk9W8GDvjX5KN9ywN9k7M905m_5PW5BWr2F6lZ3psW6BPrsr1HK8LfW2bVl3y2_jzZHW1gS0Z64GX357W8BVgg18DFtQRW4TSFsK4hw4hNW97lCDb86_GtdW7Cwxq039Px9NW4YNGdQ2ns0JKW7Q4CW61Tz08XW5GV7v04Nv8K7N2w9GTBcc3W1W4vTST53SSkRlW6s--Yh5SlDTWW55bXX58mrsTbW9gcbc_8PZzPLN74rn46WYsclW1v_WN-4pNTDMW8Mjfvs6qG7t2V9vQ9q2LKYGxVqdlJ46RwpWNN5wlWxBb6rLlVJ26qp1FSNnGW4VGqDg5RlVdTW3JsGYW6b2lV5W1Fp9WS2tdzDLW8Yx2p81PQz_fW3xKR7k6QJ7rQW32rjlM2NnTQyW2hlFfR5D495HW3bn1nr85twFdW6WMJBn5BDDfpVw_qJB54lH1BVN7qq_5NyCMXW22QGdS50mQdgf2Nkg2-04 ) course on Educative. You can access this with any Educative subscription, though I recommend Premium Plus, as it provides the best value, and you can also get it now for 55% discount.

Pro Tip — Master Spring Ecosystem as it's one of the most used frameworks in the Java world.

4. Refine Your DevOps Skills

Modern Java developers are expected to own more than just code. You should know how your applications are built, tested, deployed, and monitored.

What to learn:

- Docker and Kubernetes
- CI/CD with GitHub Actions or Jenkins
- Infrastructure as Code (IaC) with Terraform
- Observability: logs, traces, metrics with Prometheus, Grafana, Elastic Stack

And, if you need resources, I suggest going through  DevOps / Containers: Docker for Developers (https://learn.educative.io/e3t/Ctc/RG+113/cz5H404/VVFMRJ2wznFgW4h-lf27dQTk9W8GDvjX5KN9ywN9k7M8K5m_5PW50kH_H6lZ3nqW8LpfKr1CylhNW26SG-j3Y5YVwN1yFtb9jPPJqW2NSlY57yC1m7W8W48TX23-4HfW3rfyrF2_ZCckW3s6J1-2Vt8GDW4tkPD546mDMPW62m7cj6nC3TVW5Q70v97QstJMW8VDz2z4JmxsgW6g7zpP3c-lRlW10dYvc1rbZ6BVMp2tN1qspBpW61dsVN7xwTJXVRMtJV7QSxV2N8Hp3xhRNSJmN1Lc2yLBrRjjW1gWgZK4__cvvW5C5wdp2-ZVyYW1fMs845cF8gjW3mMkgR7nqYg_W4W00nC7BZGX8W3NLDmD3bSJkVW6Rr-h29bYYFpW749Nxk5FPJZWW1jbg2k6wgnf7W6td_x_5gfqTVN99x1J66zsD9V3fZ4g2PjkGvW3-yz4q52XFfQW9bLM5q4hLtXbf9353fg04 ) (+ A Practical Guide to Kubernetes if needed) course on Educative. This is a hands-on course to learn and master DevOps Skills.

5. Build Microservices with Spring Boot + Spring Cloud

Monoliths are fine until you need scale and agility. Microservices, when done right, offer flexibility. Microservices are also a must-have skill for a Java developer in this increasingly cloud computing world.

What to learn:

- Spring Cloud Config, Eureka, and Gateway
- Resilience patterns with Resilience4j
- Distributed tracing with Zipkin or Jaeger
- Service-to-service authentication with OAuth2

I also recommend that you learn essential Microservices patterns like CQRS, Circuit-breaker, Saga, etc., most of which are covered in Microservices Architecture (https://learn.educative.io/e3t/Ctc/RG+113/cz5H404/VVFMRJ2wznFgW4h-lf27dQTk9W8GDvjX5KN9ywN9k7M9j5m_5PW69t95C6lZ3nTW7jW_-K27zlLxW3v3FcK5F5s8JW33g6C75pg8CQW1rLqzZ7MRG_6W1j9C1G8GrWg5W49m0Bw523CWFW6zmNjR7GffLwN27vR3cX2btxW3dd7774DV1XbW5_zQzp3jBJZWW5t7WqH172XVwW1fKN2X3vbcQGW4wBL8b8PShwGW7fyYNb1B1CXFW1VDJtM8gST2sW96GB4t5dtJ7FN5sR7K1GlCxgVNG2k42lSWGHW5bR7363tl7RzN5wsr5L2BZ2gW5BW2hL3pRGpDW8kggyV2kgGpMW8FWcj3706kVJVvK1w395hP-rV_h0-v1HQ8clW7VNVh01WKkV9W5S1bjF3LzMqPW2RnyCW18FMfnW1vpk1y4d9g7FW3VTJbm64TJBvW8McZXY3SWHb_W2_b8m76RzJ4bN7jRv4ZSVSq_VmBghX3KPYNnW8bMykD7LZdQpW2MNrdw35HF9Kf3Z1bYH04 ) course on Educative.

6. Use Containers Daily (Docker + Kubernetes)

Containers are the new standard for packaging and running apps. Kubernetes is the de facto orchestration platform.

What to learn:

- Build efficient Dockerfiles
- Use multi-stage builds
- Helm, Kustomize, and Kubernetes manifests
- Deploy Java apps to Kubernetes with Skaffold or Tilt

And, if you need a course, join DevOps / Containers: Docker for Developers (https://learn.educative.io/e3t/Ctc/RG+113/cz5H404/VVFMRJ2wznFgW4h-lf27dQTk9W8GDvjX5KN9ywN9k7M8K5m_5PW50kH_H6lZ3nqW8LpfKr1CylhNW26SG-j3Y5YVwN1yFtb9jPPJqW2NSlY57yC1m7W8W48TX23-4HfW3rfyrF2_ZCckW3s6J1-2Vt8GDW4tkPD546mDMPW62m7cj6nC3TVW5Q70v97QstJMW8VDz2z4JmxsgW6g7zpP3c-lRlW10dYvc1rbZ6BVMp2tN1qspBpW61dsVN7xwTJXVRMtJV7QSxV2N8Hp3xhRNSJmN1Lc2yLBrRjjW1gWgZK4__cvvW5C5wdp2-ZVyYW1fMs845cF8gjW3mMkgR7nqYg_W4W00nC7BZGX8W3NLDmD3bSJkVW6Rr-h29bYYFpW749Nxk5FPJZWW1jbg2k6wgnf7W6td_x_5gfqTVN99x1J66zsD9V3fZ4g2PjkGvW3-yz4q52XFfQW9bLM5q4hLtXbf9353fg04 ) . The course is full of quizzes and exercises as well as hands-on practice. You will also learn best practices for making Dockerfiles and Compose files.

7. Get Cloud-Native (AWS, Azure, GCP)

Understanding how your app runs on the cloud is essential.

Start with AWS, then learn:

- Deploying Java apps to Elastic Beanstalk, ECS, or EKS
- Serverless Java with AWS Lambda
- Cloud-native database choices: DynamoDB, Aurora, RDS
- Observability with CloudWatch and X-Ray

And, if you need a course, join Cloud (AWS): Master the AWS Certified Solutions Architect – Associate SAA-C03 (https://learn.educative.io/e3t/Ctc/RG+113/cz5H404/VVFMRJ2wznFgW4h-lf27dQTk9W8GDvjX5KN9ywN9k7M905m_5PW5BWr2F6lZ3mTW31HQb128G1VKW3lP09Y34VYmzW6-SPNZ5R3p3QW8MJgFC4gr490W8By0JZ6zXwrNW8qjHxC39fFR5W2YSKKf7D7jjxW6D6cgJ1MN7PQTSNlW6zXMb-W6hCjWk4tQgMyW94XZq37sZNb6W8dxQ588M73CsM2Cc1xQMNMKV-3yM93Y3ljtW2_WhHb37ZrmCW5tP_Ch2BbLgjW6_dKC-21RHhwW15GbsW6PR2-sW6JwYtg40Z50vW2qXh3K3rVrd8W6cTvpK1P3kj_N8VR15Lj1xPgW1Jd9qD7BKygwVC0vC37k2bm7W91Yf7Q3kPRQdW2b1p9S8MdCx1Vt2dPt6xvTKyW5XJ2TG8SHPV3W1VjK335GbW6qN5D-h_BtN97sW2_VJdq472H-KW87mYrG33CjFDW4XvTY11LlPKXW8CDKdt9grXtPdsHWRs04 ) Exam course on Educative. It’s a great course for developers to learn about Cloud Computing with AWS in mind, and also get the most desirable cloud certification of our time.

8. Security First

Security is non-optional, and as a Java Developer, you should spend some time and energy mastering it.

Learn:

- Spring Security 7 (OAuth2, OIDC, JWT)
- API security best practices
- Secure coding: XSS, CSRF, SQL injection prevention
- Secrets management with Vault or AWS Secrets Manager

If you need a resource, you can check out Security: Web Application Security for the Everyday Software Engineer (https://learn.educative.io/e3t/Ctc/RG+113/cz5H404/VVFMRJ2wznFgW4h-lf27dQTk9W8GDvjX5KN9ywN9k7M9j5m_5PW69t95C6lZ3n5N9ln1cCWS1FXW6_Dsxx383jBZN6wF6K3z3rMwW8d1Wrb7rvqNDW8LSTv12-3SDwW4Z8jCF66fDxMW7hnl7v5Pj7B8W2q9Dct4xbk59W5Lj6Cr8lrTsyVhQ8T71nT0cCW1mYR0C4vpklgVncWth755n6SW5pXbpr7lclH5W8_jBwX51jTjzW9jylXL1cQC39W8ZZbwW2DVK5PW81l26Y38rXQmW8CCLVQ7dnb2pW5wvLJ486xB2XW8JHXBM1wvd5sVmd_3W8d0SFVW9bXQNw5jBWgHW2Xr_5r91-sxFW2GbZwG8pFhh4W4Y4GxV7TDmf4W2Msmqn9b0WHQW5G__62159Lt6W31PL-_1FxBQKVXQhNg5kdVf4W7rpwP66XlGy1N3f6xwD-Bps9W8tpTWJ2Wf18JW8l6KBD7x-FD0W3Nz3pG7TFqDbW13XjnS5GyT7bW27w3wn8S0DFlf84yHJP04 ) course on Educative. I found it very engaging and informative.

Conclusion

That’s all in this post about 8 Things Java developers should learn in 2026. As I said, 2026 is going to be a huge year for AI and cloud-native development. As a Java developer, you don’t have to pivot to data science or switch to Python — Java is evolving to embrace this new era.

The key is to stay current, build real projects, and continuously improve. Pick 3–5 items from this list and go deep in the first half of the year. Then expand.

If you want more structured guidance, join newsletters, follow modern Java-focused blogs, and take a few curated courses from platforms like Educative (https://learn.educative.io/e3t/Ctc/RG+113/cz5H404/VVFMRJ2wznFgW4h-lf27dQTk9W8GDvjX5KN9ywN9k7Mbv3prCCW8wLKSR6lZ3nHW5tnxn880sV2RW7Wrp1_48MJpwW4YKwQ711-jZBW605-Xx6xjnGKN4F49J7gVbtJN9fH22CCZGJzN6wG71j-Rn_5Vvg3kj57jP4_W5CXMGV3VjV-3W2Pyfrs68S4TjW7GyGK76dfzR-W1_cSGq2SSJDjW3LrfGT1LNRNWW5y_-gk3Kbk88W7JK30F61ZQtxW3t8yzc8nSq8lV8_pnw4q_2r7W6HGYKg6gGVH3V6KY5C27CsvSW8pSZDf5p4VkVW7w0VvJ21L3ZTW8bG88B6CchYlW8JFzkk1Tkf6lW4-Hzhk34dP3HVHFTtr91jsR4W34rfs64jTlwXW8xdBD496dld7W7MMlfJ7mYfKyf4zvZQY04 ) , which provides hands-on learning online.

Let’s make 2026 your best year yet as a Java developer.

Javin
